design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect power-up items to upgrade weapons, destroy enemies for score currency, manage limited special attacks (B items), progress through randomized stage selection followed by fixed stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between seeking power-ups/score items and avoiding enemy fire; weapon power management with hidden ammo cap that resets progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an international fighter jet through aerial combat with a large roster of character options representing different nations
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Exceptionally large character roster (8 characters from 4 nations) was unusual for 1992; character-nation pairing creates asymmetric multiplayer; randomized stage selection within constraints before fixed finale stages creates variable pacing

difficulty.lives_system: Default three lives, one additional life earned at 200,000 points
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-stage progression; seven stages total with randomized first three (selected from pool of four, one per nation) and four fixed stages; second playthrough of same stages at increased difficulty after initial completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection determines which nation's stage variant player faces (player never encounters own nation's stage); two-player mode restricts to single nation; weapon power management requires decision-making about when to activate max-power and risk ammo depletion

progression.upgrade_system: P items upgrade normal weapons (fire button); maximum power level has hidden ammo count, triggering reset to previous power level when depleted; F items (rare) also upgrade weapons; B items provide special attacks (button 2), limited by B item collection
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character/nation selection determines stage route; beating all seven stages unlocks character-specific ending and second playthrough with more difficult stage variants
replayability_hooks: Eight character roster with distinct nation pairings encourages multiple playthroughs
Randomized stage selection (first three stages) creates variable routes
Multiplayer mode forces single-nation choice, creating different experience than single-player
Score-based progression (200k point bonus life) rewards high-score chasing
Hidden ammo cap mechanic adds strategic depth to power management

world_building_techniques: Character names and nationalities (US, Japan, Sweden, UK) ground pilots in geopolitical context
Real-world fighter jet models (F/A-18 Hornet, F-14 Tomcat, FSX, F-15 Eagle, Viggen, Gripen, Harrier II, Tornado) provide military authenticity
Character-specific endings provide minimal narrative payoff

faction_indicators: United Kingdom (Villiam Syd Pride, Lord River N. White)
environmental_storytelling: Nation-specific stage variants determine visual/thematic setting of first three stages
Real military aircraft create technical authenticity signaling
Character nationality expressed through pilot names and aircraft selection
